f_a18_2: PASS (encode produced, decode not run separately)
note: 23.8x worse than the ~6300 ms/KB spec baseline; 47x over the 500 ms/KB target.
22x over 500 ms/KB target. Different latency curve from text-heavy hive corpus — atlas has fewer LZ matches.

verdict: INTERP_KILL — process killed (likely OOM/watchdog) before encode finished. Confirms super-linear interpreter latency curve. 79KB n6 atlas (originally requested) extrapolates to >10 min interpreter-encode and is NOT viable to measure on this host without AOT.

root_cause: codegen_c2_v2.c emits hexa_call2(map_contains_key, ...) for bare-call form but only the method form (.contains_key / .keys) has a special-case in the codegen. Bare-call builtins are NOT registered in the C runtime, so clang aborts with use-of-undeclared-identifier.
bug2_aot_cache_status: orthogonal — this is a separate codegen builtin coverage gap, NOT the source_key path-canonicalization fix (Bug 2 verdict commit_pending).

note: A17 (companion order-3 module) fails identically — 12 errors generated. Confirms the gap is hexa-AOT-toolchain-wide, not A18-specific. Survey: A17=12, A18=10, A19=1 bare-call usages; A1-A16 + A20+ use 0 (probably already method-form converted).

reason_aot_unmeasured: AOT build blocked at codegen builtin coverage gap; cannot produce a native binary to measure against the 500 ms/KB ceiling.
expected_aot_speedup: hexa AOT typically delivers 10x-100x over interpreter for compute-heavy hexa code. If 10x: 24k → 2400 ms/KB (still FAIL). If 100x: 24k → 240 ms/KB (PASS). The AOT verdict cannot be predicted without measurement.

name: interpreter-vs-AOT latency dichotomy
statement: F-A18-3 (and analogous per-module latency falsifiers) was specified without naming the runtime axis. Interpreter and AOT latency differ by 1-2 orders of magnitude on hexa. A pre-registered falsifier that does not name its measurement axis is operationally underspecified — a single number can both PASS and FAIL depending on which binary executed it. Strengthening: every Phase-10 latency falsifier MUST name {INTERP, AOT, both} as its measurement scope at preregistration time. Existing falsifiers MUST be retroactively classified.
action: raw 137 strengthening applied to A18 module header (see commit). Sweep of A1-A17 latency falsifiers deferred to a separate ω-cycle.
